{{Quote|The strong survive, the weak die. That's the way the galaxy works. The day we forgot that, we became everyone's lackey.|Dred Priest|<ref name="Imperial Commando: 501st">''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]''</ref>}}
'''Dred Priest''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior who was accepted into [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett's]] invitation to train [[Clone commando/Legends|clone commandos]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] as part of the ''[[Cuy'val Dar]]''. During his time on Kamino, Priest earned the ire of some of his fellow Mandalorian ''Cuy'val Dar''. [[Kal Skirata]] and [[Walon Vau]] abhorred his [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]] leanings which taught that Mandalorians ought to restore their [[Mandalorian Wars|old]] glory by conquering [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Another Mandalorian training sergeant, [[Mij Gilamar]], loathed Priest because of his formation of a [[Battle Circle]], where his clone trainees engaged in deadly fights. Gilamar, who also served as a [[Doctor/Legends|doctor]], had to treat Priest's injured trainees and saw many that were beyond treatment and later [[Death/Legends|died]].

A few months after the formation of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Priest, along with his girlfriend [[Isabet Reau]], were part of the resurgent Death Watch. The pair were stationed on [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]] and were actively working with the Imperial garrison. When Gilamar and Kal's adopted son [[Ordo Skirata]] encountered Priest in the city of [[Keldabe/Legends|Keldabe]], Priest tried to recruit Gilamar into joining the Death Watch. Upon confirming his belief that Priest was still intent in having the Mandalorians rebuild their old empire, Gilamar stabbed Priest with a [[Knife/Legends|knife]] above his thigh, slashing through Priest's femoral artery and causing massive blood loss. Priest died quickly and his body was disposed of by Gilamar and Ordo who threw his body into the [[Kelita River]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Former Cuy'val Dar. One of Jango Fett's less inspired choices for training sergeant—good soldier, but a complete nutter.|Ordo Skirata on Dred Priest|<ref name="Imperial Commando: 501st" />}}
Dred Priest was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] and part of the one hundred training [[Sergeant/Legends|sergeants]], known as the ''[[Cuy'val Dar]]'', that [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] selected to help train [[Clone commando/Legends|clone commandos]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]. Priest accepted the invitation and relished the challenge that creating elite soldiers offered. During his stay at Kamino, he secretly formed a [[Battle Circle]] with [[Isabet Reau]]—a female ''Cuy'val Dar'' whom he soon started a relationship with—where they forced their trainees to fight each other in brutal competitions. Due to the injuries suffered by the clone trainees, [[Mij Gilamar]]—another ''Cuy'val Dar'' and the [[Doctor/Legends|doctor]] who treated the injured clones— informed Fett of the situation, who put a violent end to Priest's fight club. Despite that incident and their [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]] leanings, Fett allowed them to stay as he was more concerned of their training results.

The ''[[Ba'jurne Kyr'tsad Mando'ad]]'', a manifesto of the Death Watch Mandalorian splinter group, was supposedly [[Author/Legends|written]] by the movement's [[Secret Mandalore|leader]], [[Tor Vizsla]]—although in one of his notes the Death Watch's opponent Jango Fett doubted its authenticity and suggested that Dred Priest or Isabet Reau wrote the manifesto—and was eventually included in the [[Book/Legends|book]] ''[[The Bounty Hunter Code]]'' by Jango's son, the bounty hunter [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]].<ref name="TBHC">''[[The Bounty Hunter Code: From the Files of Boba Fett]]''</ref>

===Resurgence===
{{Quote|Lorka Gedyc has big plans for us. Forget your petty personal squabbles with the aruetyc Empire and start thinking about our rightful heritage. We weren't always the aruetiise's latrine-cleaners. We've got the beskar—and we can use it.|Dred Priest|<ref name="Imperial Commando: 501st" />}}
In [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]], a year after the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] and the formation of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Priest and Reau were on the planet [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]] working with the local Imperial garrison and recruiting for the revived Death Watch. Priest ran into Gilamar and [[Null-class Advanced Recon Commando|Null-ARC]] [[Ordo Skirata]], and tried to convince them to join the Death Watch. They led him down into an underground chasm where they talked further. Upon realizing that Priest had not changed and was still firm in his belief of establishing a Mandalorian empire with the Death Watch, Gilamar stabbed him in the leg with his [[Knife/Legends|knife]], severing his femoral artery and swiftly killing Priest from severe blood loss. Gilamar and Skirata tossed his corpse into the churning [[Kelita River]] to both delay Reau from discovering his fate and to hide who really killed him.

==Personality and traits==
Dred Priest was a firm believer in the Mandalorians ruling the galaxy like their forebears of old. Mij Gilamar described Priest as a merciless butcher, making his trainees fight each other on the battle circle for his amusement.

Priest had a thin face with a lopsided mouth, the sort of face one could punch all day, according to Kal Skirata.

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:FullArmorDredPriest.jpg|thumb|left|Dred Priest in full armor]]
This figure uses parts from 2004's Vintage Original Trilogy Collection [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett's]] arms and legs, along with a new head, belt, and torso.

Like the [[Rav Bralor]] figure in the set, Dred Priest appears to be loosely based on artwork done by [[Tom Hodges]] for the [[Star Wars Insider 86|''Star Wars Insider'' 86]] article ''[[The Mandalorians: People and Culture]]''. In [[:File:Mandalorian_wedding.jpg|this picture]] you can see that the waist and shoulder ammo belts, cod piece, and upper torso armor are identical. However, the helmet on the action figure is not as intricately painted as the one in the artwork, nor does the action figure have any thigh and shin armor. The legs are taken from a Boba Fett action figure.

The head under the helmet is loosely based on Hasbro employee Derryl Depriest, whose name also served as a basis for the figure.
